title: Limnochloa media
intro: Limnochloa media, also known as Cyperus rotundus, is a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Limnochloa media include alkaloids, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limnochloa media include teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
